# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](../AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

**feat: message pool allocator**

Todo #1 picked up: decouple "get a message" from "send it"
with a message pool — self-describing attach-validated
region, fixed-size cache-aligned buffers, intrusive LIFO
free-stack (single-popper Treiber, validated pops), one
owning allocator, anyone frees. Strive for simplicity: the
smallest API that lets us hone the shape and gives iiac-perf
a baseline —

- buffer header is the next-link alone for now; provenance
  (pool id) and any length/type tag land with the
  descriptor-queue cycle, via a pool layout_version bump
  (conscious deferral of the design doc's settle-early note).
- API mirrors the ring: `Pool::init` / `unsafe Pool::attach`,
  a single allocator handle, typed zerocopy access to
  buffers.

Ladder:

- 0.6.0-0 chore: open message pool cycle (done)
- 0.6.0-1 feat: message pool layout + init/attach (done)
- 0.6.0-2 feat: message pool alloc/free (done)
- 0.6.0-3 docs: message pool usage model — roles (ring:
  one producer + one consumer; pool: one allocator + any
  freers), buffer lifecycle states (free → allocated →
  in-flight → freed) and who may touch a buffer in each,
  what "send" means before descriptor queues exist (done)
- 0.6.0-4 test: message pool protocol tests
- 0.6.0 feat: message pool allocator (close-out)

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](../AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. Descriptor queues: carry `(pool id, buffer offset)`
   descriptors over the existing SPSC ring so any message
   travels over any queue; per-process pool registry to
   resolve ids
   [details](ring-buffer-design.md#messaging-layer-pools-and-descriptor-queues).
2. Endpoint claims word: CAS-claimed producer/consumer roles
   in the ring header so a second attach/split claimant gets
   an error instead of silently violating SPSC; costs a
   layout_version bump (or spends `_pad0`)
   [details](ring-buffer-design.md#resolved-questions).
3. Typed endpoints: `Producer<T>` / `Consumer<T>` validating
   `T`'s geometry once at split instead of asserting on every
   reserve_slot [details](ring-buffer-design.md#api).

## Ideas

- Perf benches live in
  [iiac-perf](https://github.com/winksaville/iiac-perf)
  (sibling repo `../iiac-perf`), not here — its calibrated
  harness compares zc-ring against mpsc et al. directly
  (`zcring-1t`/`zcring-2t` mirroring `mpsc_1t`/`mpsc_2t`).
  An in-repo bench only if per-commit regression tracking
  proves necessary.

- Study [iceoryx2](https://github.com/eclipse-iceoryx/iceoryx2)
  before implementing message pools — battle-tested loan/send
  decoupling and pool-offset machinery; how it differs from
  this project in
  [Prior art: iceoryx2](ring-buffer-design.md#prior-art-iceoryx2).
- BufSlot auto-free on Drop (RAII, iceoryx2-style): kills the
  silent leak-on-drop footgun at the cost of guard-type
  asymmetry (ring guards' drop = do-nothing) and a
  ManuallyDrop dance in free/send paths. Decide when
  descriptor-queue send lands — explicit free is easier to
  upgrade than to walk back.
- Overflow FIFO: when a queue's ring is Full, append the
  message to a sender-private intrusive pending list (same
  embedded next-link the free-stack uses; zero allocation,
  naturally bounded by pool capacity)
  [details](ring-buffer-design.md#overflow-fifo-future).
- Blocking layer above the crate (futex, eventfd, async
  wakers) built on the header's user line — mechanism and
  contracts in
  [Blocking and user words](ring-buffer-design.md#blocking-and-user-words);
  possibly a companion wrapper crate so independent peers
  share one protocol.
- loom-based exhaustive ordering exploration of the SPSC
  protocol.
- Polish: `Error` implements `Display` + `core::error::Error`;
  `occupancy()` / `is_empty()` accessors.
- Packed-slot variant (drop the cache-line-multiple slot
  constraint) for small-message space efficiency.
- Per-target / configurable `CACHE_LINE` (128 for Apple
  M-series false sharing, tiny for cache-less MCUs) — safe
  since attach validates the header's `cache_line`; decide
  values from iiac-perf measurements.
- Embedded floor: protocol is atomic load/store only (no
  CAS), so thumbv6m works today; keep it that way where
  possible (endpoint claims wants CAS — gate it), and 8/16-bit
  targets would need index-width genericization.
- Shared `Geometry` struct (`slot_size`, `capacity`, `mask`)
  held by Ring and passed whole to the endpoint
  constructors, slimming their signatures and Ring's fields.
- Black-box test split: move the public-API protocol tests
  (roundtrip, abandoned guards, threaded stress) to
  `tests/protocol.rs`; white-box tests (u32 wrap, attach
  header internals) stay in lib.rs. Do it when a trybuild
  compile-fail harness lands there too (pins the
  "second reserve_slot does not compile" guarantee).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

content.text:
All three angles are in. On the **global allocator** question specifically — not as-is, but the path exists:

- `GlobalAlloc` demands `alloc(&self)` callable from **every thread concurrently**; our pool's whole phase-1 contract is one popper holding `&mut self`. Bridging that needs either the phase-2 shared-allocation pools (the generation-tagged head CAS we designed) or per-thread pools behind a routing layer — which is exactly how production mallocs are built (thread-local caches over shared slabs), so the arc is well-trodden.
- `GlobalAlloc` must serve **arbitrary `Layout`s**; a pool serves one size class. A real global allocator would be a small family of pools (64 B, 256 B, 4 KiB, …) with size-class routing and an oversize fallback.
- The part that already fits perfectly: `dealloc` from any thread is our MPSC free push, no adaptation needed.

So: feasible as a follow-on experiment, recorded as an Ideas entry with those constraints spelled out (and a note to measure the plain object-pool form in iiac-perf first — the numbers will say whether the malloc arc is worth walking).

README changes beyond that: the usage model's new **"Where this is heading"** block — a clearly-marked illustrative sketch of the composed flow (alloc → fill in place → `send` consumes the guard, index rides the ring → receiver resolves a zero-copy view → free returns it), closing with the one-sentence thesis: *bytes written once, never copied — not by send, not by receive, not across the process boundary*. Plus a Message-pool bullet making the standalone value explicit: a lock-free, syscall-free, O(1) fixed-size object allocator with LIFO cache reuse, usable today for objects that never travel.

The `-3` description I drafted is now stale — want me to rewrite it to cover the expanded content, or do you have more README reactions first?

The decoupled ownership API: alloc pops the first free
buffer as a BufSlot owned until free — the guard does not
borrow the pool, so any number of allocated buffers live at
once, and getting a message implies nothing about when it
is sent or freed. Validated Treiber ops: single-popper pop
(no ABA), MPSC Release push, corruption fails toward
Exhausted, never out of bounds.

- src/pool.rs
  - Exhausted: alloc failure (empty or peer-corrupted
    stack — fail-safe like the ring's Full)
  - Pool::alloc<T>: Acquire + bounds-checked head and
    next-link, CAS pop; &mut self as the in-process
    single-popper token; returns BufSlot<'a, T> tied to
    the region lifetime, not the handle borrow
  - BufSlot<T>: Deref/DerefMut mint refs per access from a
    raw pointer (ring guards' aliasing pattern); free(self)
    re-links the first word and Release-CAS pushes; drop
    without free leaks (documented); Send when T: Send

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](../AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

**feat: message pool allocator**

Todo #1 picked up: decouple "get a message" from "send it"
with a message pool — self-describing attach-validated
region, fixed-size cache-aligned buffers, intrusive LIFO
free-stack (single-popper Treiber, validated pops), one
owning allocator, anyone frees. Strive for simplicity: the
smallest API that lets us hone the shape and gives iiac-perf
a baseline —

- buffer header is the next-link alone for now; provenance
  (pool id) and any length/type tag land with the
  descriptor-queue cycle, via a pool layout_version bump
  (conscious deferral of the design doc's settle-early note).
- API mirrors the ring: `Pool::init` / `unsafe Pool::attach`,
  a single allocator handle, typed zerocopy access to
  buffers.

Ladder:

- 0.6.0-0 chore: open message pool cycle (done)
- 0.6.0-1 feat: message pool layout + init/attach (done)
- 0.6.0-2 feat: message pool alloc/free (done)
- 0.6.0-3 docs: message pool usage model — roles (ring:
  one producer + one consumer; pool: one allocator + any
  freers), buffer lifecycle states (free → allocated →
  in-flight → freed) and who may touch a buffer in each,
  what "send" means before descriptor queues exist (done)
- 0.6.0-4 test: message pool protocol tests
- 0.6.0 feat: message pool allocator (close-out)

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](../AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. Descriptor queues: carry `(pool id, buffer offset)`
   descriptors over the existing SPSC ring so any message
   travels over any queue; per-process pool registry to
   resolve ids
   [details](ring-buffer-design.md#messaging-layer-pools-and-descriptor-queues).
2. Endpoint claims word: CAS-claimed producer/consumer roles
   in the ring header so a second attach/split claimant gets
   an error instead of silently violating SPSC; costs a
   layout_version bump (or spends `_pad0`)
   [details](ring-buffer-design.md#resolved-questions).
3. Typed endpoints: `Producer<T>` / `Consumer<T>` validating
   `T`'s geometry once at split instead of asserting on every
   reserve_slot [details](ring-buffer-design.md#api).

## Ideas

- Perf benches live in
  [iiac-perf](https://github.com/winksaville/iiac-perf)
  (sibling repo `../iiac-perf`), not here — its calibrated
  harness compares zc-ring against mpsc et al. directly
  (`zcring-1t`/`zcring-2t` mirroring `mpsc_1t`/`mpsc_2t`).
  An in-repo bench only if per-commit regression tracking
  proves necessary.

- Study [iceoryx2](https://github.com/eclipse-iceoryx/iceoryx2)
  before implementing message pools — battle-tested loan/send
  decoupling and pool-offset machinery; how it differs from
  this project in
  [Prior art: iceoryx2](ring-buffer-design.md#prior-art-iceoryx2).
- `#[global_allocator]` experiment over size-class pools:
  GlobalAlloc is `&self` + any-thread, so it needs
  shared-allocation pools (phase 2 gen-tagged head) or
  per-thread pools with a routing layer; arbitrary `Layout`
  needs size-class selection + an oversize fallback. Frees
  from any thread are already natural (MPSC push). Classic
  mempool → malloc arc; measure the object-pool form in
  iiac-perf first.
- BufSlot auto-free on Drop (RAII, iceoryx2-style): kills the
  silent leak-on-drop footgun at the cost of guard-type
  asymmetry (ring guards' drop = do-nothing) and a
  ManuallyDrop dance in free/send paths. Decide when
  descriptor-queue send lands — explicit free is easier to
  upgrade than to walk back.
- Overflow FIFO: when a queue's ring is Full, append the
  message to a sender-private intrusive pending list (same
  embedded next-link the free-stack uses; zero allocation,
  naturally bounded by pool capacity)
  [details](ring-buffer-design.md#overflow-fifo-future).
- Blocking layer above the crate (futex, eventfd, async
  wakers) built on the header's user line — mechanism and
  contracts in
  [Blocking and user words](ring-buffer-design.md#blocking-and-user-words);
  possibly a companion wrapper crate so independent peers
  share one protocol.
- loom-based exhaustive ordering exploration of the SPSC
  protocol.
- Polish: `Error` implements `Display` + `core::error::Error`;
  `occupancy()` / `is_empty()` accessors.
- Packed-slot variant (drop the cache-line-multiple slot
  constraint) for small-message space efficiency.
- Per-target / configurable `CACHE_LINE` (128 for Apple
  M-series false sharing, tiny for cache-less MCUs) — safe
  since attach validates the header's `cache_line`; decide
  values from iiac-perf measurements.
- Embedded floor: protocol is atomic load/store only (no
  CAS), so thumbv6m works today; keep it that way where
  possible (endpoint claims wants CAS — gate it), and 8/16-bit
  targets would need index-width genericization.
- Shared `Geometry` struct (`slot_size`, `capacity`, `mask`)
  held by Ring and passed whole to the endpoint
  constructors, slimming their signatures and Ring's fields.
- Black-box test split: move the public-API protocol tests
  (roundtrip, abandoned guards, threaded stress) to
  `tests/protocol.rs`; white-box tests (u32 wrap, attach
  header internals) stay in lib.rs. Do it when a trybuild
  compile-fail harness lands there too (pins the
  "second reserve_slot does not compile" guarantee).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- docs: messaging layer design (pools + queues) [[6]]

[6]: chores/chores-01.md#docs-messaging-layer-design-pools--queues

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](../AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

**feat: message pool allocator**

Todo #1 picked up: decouple "get a message" from "send it"
with a message pool — self-describing attach-validated
region, fixed-size cache-aligned buffers, intrusive LIFO
free-stack (single-popper Treiber, validated pops), one
owning allocator, anyone frees. Strive for simplicity: the
smallest API that lets us hone the shape and gives iiac-perf
a baseline —

- buffer header is the next-link alone for now; provenance
  (pool id) and any length/type tag land with the
  descriptor-queue cycle, via a pool layout_version bump
  (conscious deferral of the design doc's settle-early note).
- API mirrors the ring: `Pool::init` / `unsafe Pool::attach`,
  a single allocator handle, typed zerocopy access to
  buffers.

Ladder:

- 0.6.0-0 chore: open message pool cycle (done)
- 0.6.0-1 feat: message pool layout + init/attach (done)
- 0.6.0-2 feat: message pool alloc/free (done)
- 0.6.0-3 docs: message pool usage model — roles (ring:
  one producer + one consumer; pool: one allocator + any
  freers), buffer lifecycle states (free → allocated →
  in-flight → freed) and who may touch a buffer in each,
  what "send" means before descriptor queues exist (done)
- 0.6.0-4 test: message pool protocol tests (current)
- 0.6.0 feat: message pool allocator (close-out)

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](../AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. Descriptor queues: carry `(pool id, buffer offset)`
   descriptors over the existing SPSC ring so any message
   travels over any queue; per-process pool registry to
   resolve ids
   [details](ring-buffer-design.md#messaging-layer-pools-and-descriptor-queues).
2. Endpoint claims word: CAS-claimed producer/consumer roles
   in the ring header so a second attach/split claimant gets
   an error instead of silently violating SPSC; costs a
   layout_version bump (or spends `_pad0`)
   [details](ring-buffer-design.md#resolved-questions).
3. Typed endpoints: `Producer<T>` / `Consumer<T>` validating
   `T`'s geometry once at split instead of asserting on every
   reserve_slot [details](ring-buffer-design.md#api).

## Ideas

- Perf benches live in
  [iiac-perf](https://github.com/winksaville/iiac-perf)
  (sibling repo `../iiac-perf`), not here — its calibrated
  harness compares zc-ring against mpsc et al. directly
  (`zcring-1t`/`zcring-2t` mirroring `mpsc_1t`/`mpsc_2t`).
  An in-repo bench only if per-commit regression tracking
  proves necessary.

- Study [iceoryx2](https://github.com/eclipse-iceoryx/iceoryx2)
  before implementing message pools — battle-tested loan/send
  decoupling and pool-offset machinery; how it differs from
  this project in
  [Prior art: iceoryx2](ring-buffer-design.md#prior-art-iceoryx2).
- `#[global_allocator]` experiment over size-class pools:
  GlobalAlloc is `&self` + any-thread, so it needs
  shared-allocation pools (phase 2 gen-tagged head) or
  per-thread pools with a routing layer; arbitrary `Layout`
  needs size-class selection + an oversize fallback. Frees
  from any thread are already natural (MPSC push). Classic
  mempool → malloc arc; measure the object-pool form in
  iiac-perf first.
- BufSlot auto-free on Drop (RAII, iceoryx2-style): kills the
  silent leak-on-drop footgun at the cost of guard-type
  asymmetry (ring guards' drop = do-nothing) and a
  ManuallyDrop dance in free/send paths. Decide when
  descriptor-queue send lands — explicit free is easier to
  upgrade than to walk back.
- Overflow FIFO: when a queue's ring is Full, append the
  message to a sender-private intrusive pending list (same
  embedded next-link the free-stack uses; zero allocation,
  naturally bounded by pool capacity)
  [details](ring-buffer-design.md#overflow-fifo-future).
- Blocking layer above the crate (futex, eventfd, async
  wakers) built on the header's user line — mechanism and
  contracts in
  [Blocking and user words](ring-buffer-design.md#blocking-and-user-words);
  possibly a companion wrapper crate so independent peers
  share one protocol.
- loom-based exhaustive ordering exploration of the SPSC
  protocol.
- Polish: `Error` implements `Display` + `core::error::Error`;
  `occupancy()` / `is_empty()` accessors.
- Packed-slot variant (drop the cache-line-multiple slot
  constraint) for small-message space efficiency.
- Per-target / configurable `CACHE_LINE` (128 for Apple
  M-series false sharing, tiny for cache-less MCUs) — safe
  since attach validates the header's `cache_line`; decide
  values from iiac-perf measurements.
- Embedded floor: protocol is atomic load/store only (no
  CAS), so thumbv6m works today; keep it that way where
  possible (endpoint claims wants CAS — gate it), and 8/16-bit
  targets would need index-width genericization.
- Shared `Geometry` struct (`slot_size`, `capacity`, `mask`)
  held by Ring and passed whole to the endpoint
  constructors, slimming their signatures and Ring's fields.
- Black-box test split: move the public-API protocol tests
  (roundtrip, abandoned guards, threaded stress) to
  `tests/protocol.rs`; white-box tests (u32 wrap, attach
  header internals) stay in lib.rs. Do it when a trybuild
  compile-fail harness lands there too (pins the
  "second reserve_slot does not compile" guarantee).

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- feat: ring buffer user line + blocking contract [[5]]
- docs: messaging layer design (pools + queues) [[6]]

# References

[5]: chores/chores-01.md#feat-ring-buffer-user-line--blocking-contract
[6]: chores/chores-01.md#docs-messaging-layer-design-pools--queues

content.text:
`0.6.0-4 test: message pool protocol tests` is ready to commit — 7 new tests in `pool.rs`, each pinned to a clause of the usage model, all passing under `cargo test` and full Miri (14 total with the ring's):

- **`init_rejects_bad_geometry`** — BadBufSize/BadBufCount/TooSmall/Misaligned, plus the 32-bit `size × count` wrap tripwire, mirroring the ring's.
- **`attach_validates_header`** — uninitialized → `BadMagic`; **a ring region → `BadMagic`** (the kinds can never cross-attach — a test only possible because the magics differ); good attach snapshots geometry; scribbled `cache_line_size` → `BadCacheLine`.
- **`alloc_holds_many_then_exhausts`** — the decoupling itself: four guards live at once with intact distinct contents, fifth alloc `Exhausted`, full recycle after frees.
- **`free_is_lifo`** — pointer-identity proof that the last freed buffer is the next allocated.
- **`dropped_guard_leaks_its_buffer`** — drop-without-free leaks exactly one buffer, pool serves `count-1` thereafter.
- **`corrupted_stack_fails_toward_exhausted`** — hostile head → `Exhausted`; valid head with hostile next-link → `Exhausted` (both validation arms); repaired link restores service — degraded, never bricked, never out-of-bounds.
- **`threaded_alloc_here_free_there`** — the role split live: allocator thread allocs and hands guards over a channel, freer thread verifies and frees; 100k messages through 4 buffers (200 under Miri), exercising the CAS push/pop pairing and `BufSlot: Send`.

One implementation addition surfaced by the threaded test: `Pool` itself now has `unsafe impl Send` (the allocator role moving to another thread), same rationale and placement as the ring endpoints' — the raw `*mut u8` field was blocking an otherwise-legitimate move.

Bookkeeping rode along: `[[18]]` backfill (`-3` SHA `9f3ce4bc…`), ladder `-4` done, version `0.6.0-4`. Ready for the description.

message.content: Make use a const for cache size in the tests, and test different sizes. Test different sizes on the same buffer and test types with internal alignment work.

Use a const for cache size in the tests, and test different sizes. Test different sized types in the same buffer and test that types with internal alignment work. Actually, how do we handle types that don't fit. What about allocating a zero-sized type?

content.text:
Good push — those are exactly the API edges this cycle should pin down. Direct answers first, then the tests:

- **Types that don't fit**: `check_type` panics ("programming error, not runtime condition" — same doctrine as the ring's `reserve_slot`). That's currently only documented; I'll pin it with a `#[should_panic]` test.
- **ZSTs**: they're legal (`size 0 ≤ buf_size`, align 1) and *consume a whole buffer* — the buffer is the allocation granule. Weird but consistent; I'll document it on `alloc` and test it.

attachment.snippet:
1	//! Message pool: fixed-size buffers over a caller-provided
2	//! region, handed out through an intrusive LIFO free-stack —
3	//! the allocation half of the messaging layer, decoupling
4	//! "get a message" from "send it" (see the design doc's
5	//! "Messaging layer: pools and descriptor queues").
6	//!
7	//! - One `#[repr(C)]` [`PoolHeader`] spanning two cache lines
8	//!   (immutable geometry, free-stack head), followed by
9	//!   `buf_count` buffers of `buf_size` bytes each.
10	//! - The free-stack is intrusive with zero per-buffer
11	//!   overhead: a *free* buffer's first word holds the index
12	//!   of the next free buffer; an allocated buffer carries no
13	//!   header at all (pool-id provenance is deferred — see
14	//!   todo.md's In Progress block).
15	//! - One owning allocator pops ([`Pool::alloc`], `&mut self`
16	//!   as the in-process single-popper token); any holder
17	//!   frees ([`BufSlot::free`], the MPSC push side). The
18	//!   [`BufSlot`] guard does not borrow the pool, so any
19	//!   number of allocated buffers may be live at once —
20	//!   the decoupling the messaging layer exists for.
21	
22	use core::marker::PhantomData;
23	use core::mem::size_of;
24	use core::ops::{Deref, DerefMut};
25	use core::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, Ordering};
26	use zerocopy::{FromBytes, IntoBytes, KnownLayout};
27	
28	use crate::{CACHE_LINE, CacheAligned, Error, check_type};
29	
30	/// `alloc` failed: no free buffer (or a peer-corrupted
31	/// free-stack index — validation fails toward exhaustion,
32	/// never toward handing out memory the pool doesn't own).
33	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
34	pub struct Exhausted;
35	
36	/// Layout marker written by [`Pool::init`]; rejects foreign
37	/// (including ring) regions on attach.
38	const POOL_MAGIC: u32 = 0x5A43_5031; // "ZCP1"
39	
40	/// Bumped on any change to the pool region layout.
41	const POOL_LAYOUT_VERSION: u32 = 1;
42	
43	/// Free-stack terminator: no next free buffer.
44	///
45	/// - Also why `buf_count < u32::MAX`: every valid index must
46	///   be distinguishable from the sentinel.
47	const NIL: u32 = u32::MAX;
48	
49	/// Control block at offset 0 of a pool region — two cache
50	/// lines: cold geometry, then the contended free-stack head.
51	///
52	/// - line 0: geometry, written by [`Pool::init`] with `magic`
53	///   last (`Release`), read-only thereafter. Cold: per-op
54	///   paths use the handle's snapshot.
55	/// - line 1: `first_free_idx`, CAS-contended by every freer and
56	///   the allocator — sole owner of its line.
57	/// - Every field is atomic for the same reason as the ring
58	///   [`Header`](crate::Header): a peer may be mapped at any
59	///   time, and scribbles must be garbage values, never UB.
60	#[repr(C)]
61	pub struct PoolHeader {
62	    /// Layout marker ([`POOL_MAGIC`]); stored last by init
63	    /// (`Release`), loaded first by attach (`Acquire`).
64	    magic: AtomicU32,
65	    /// Pool layout version ([`POOL_LAYOUT_VERSION`]).
66	    layout_version: AtomicU32,
67	    /// Buffer size in bytes — a [`CACHE_LINE`] multiple.
68	    buf_size: AtomicU32,
69	    /// Buffer count — nonzero, `< u32::MAX` ([`NIL`]).
70	    buf_count: AtomicU32,
71	    /// [`CACHE_LINE`] this region was built with; attach
72	    /// validates it like the rest of the geometry.
73	    cache_line_size: AtomicU32,
74	    /// Free-stack head: index of the first free buffer, or
75	    /// [`NIL`] when the pool is exhausted.
76	    first_free_idx: CacheAligned<AtomicU32>,
77	}
78	
79	const _: () = assert!(size_of::<PoolHeader>() == 2 * CACHE_LINE);
80	
81	/// A validated view over a pool region.
82	///
83	/// - Geometry is snapshotted out of the header at
84	///   init/attach: per-op paths never re-read fields a peer
85	///   could scribble on. Free-stack links live in shared
86	///   buffer memory and are validated at every pop instead.
87	pub struct Pool<'a> {
88	    /// The region's control block.
89	    header: &'a PoolHeader,
90	    /// Base of the buffer array
91	    /// (offset `size_of::<PoolHeader>()`).
92	    bufs: *mut u8,
93	    /// Snapshot of `header.buf_size`.
94	    buf_size: u32,
95	    /// Snapshot of `header.buf_count`.
96	    buf_count: u32,
97	    _region: PhantomData<&'a [u8]>,
98	}
99	
100	// SAFETY: the handle owns the allocator role; the shared
101	// state it touches (first_free_idx, next-links) is atomic,
102	// and buffer ownership is handed off with Release/Acquire
103	// ordering — same rationale as the ring endpoints.
104	unsafe impl Send for Pool<'_> {}
105	
106	impl<'a> Pool<'a> {
107	    /// Initialize a fresh region and return the pool over it,
108	    /// with every buffer on the free-stack.
109	    ///
110	    /// - `buf_size` — bytes per buffer, a [`CACHE_LINE`]
111	    ///   multiple.
112	    /// - `buf_count` — number of buffers, nonzero and
113	    ///   `< u32::MAX`.
114	    /// - The region must be [`CACHE_LINE`]-aligned and at
115	    ///   least `size_of::<PoolHeader>() + buf_count *
116	    ///   buf_size` bytes.

content.text:
All green — 18 tests under `cargo test` and full Miri. The four additions per your requests:

- **`wider_buffers_and_mixed_types`** — a 128-byte-buffer pool: a 16-byte `Msg` and a buffer-filling `Big` (`[u64; 16]`) live in the pool simultaneously, the full span of the wide buffer is writable, and a freed buffer is *reused as a different type* (pointer-identity checked). Buffer size expressed via `2 * CACHE_LINE`, no literals.
- **`internally_aligned_types_work`** — a `#[repr(C, align(32))]` type: allocates, pointer verified 32-aligned (any `align ≤ CACHE_LINE` is satisfied by construction since buffer bases are line-aligned), round-trips.
- **`too_big_type_panics`** — `#[should_panic]` pinning the doesn't-fit answer: a two-line type on a one-line pool panics in `check_type` at the `alloc` call.
- **`zero_sized_type_consumes_a_buffer`** — four ZST allocs exhaust a four-buffer pool: legal, granule-sized, documented now in `alloc`'s doc comment too.

Plus the const sweep: every literal `64` in the pool tests is now `BUF` (= `CACHE_LINE as u32`) or `CACHE_LINE` arithmetic; only the `#[repr(align(64))]` attributes keep the literal (attribute position can't name a const — same limitation the ring documents).

One small honesty note: the panic message the `should_panic` pins is `"T larger than slot"` — `check_type` is shared with the ring, so "slot" is the ring's word even in a pool context. Genericizing the message ("T larger than buffer/slot") is a two-line change if it bothers you; otherwise the doctrine and the test stand as-is. Ready for the description either way.

message.content: Let's use TEST_CACHE_LINE, also I lean toward CACHE_LINE_LEN or CACHE_LINE_SIZE, thoughts_
Let's use TEST_CACHE_LINE, also I lean toward CACHE_LINE_LEN or CACHE_LINE_SIZE or CLL if you want to be shoooort, thoughts_

content.text:
My thoughts: **`CACHE_LINE_SIZE`** wins, and here's the ranking logic:

- **`CACHE_LINE_SIZE`** — matches the header field we just renamed (`cache_line_size` records this constant; name symmetry makes that relationship self-evident), and "size" is Rust's word for bytes (`size_of`).
- **`CACHE_LINE_LEN`** — in Rust, `len` connotes element count (`slice.len()`); this is bytes, so `SIZE` over `LEN`.
- **`CLL`** — vetoed by the repo's own rule: AGENTS.md bans decoder-ring abbreviations ("Abbreviations (e.g. `SE`) are not used because they require a decoder ring"). Shoooort loses to readable here :)

And `TEST_CACHE_LINE` for the test const — one nuance: that const's actual role is the *buffer size* the tests pass to `Pool::init` (which happens to be one cache line). I'll name it as you asked; if it ever reads odd as `Pool::init(&mut r.0, TEST_CACHE_LINE, 4)`, the alternative is `TEST_BUF_SIZE`. Applying both renames:

1	//! Message pool: fixed-size buffers over a caller-provided
2	//! region, handed out through an intrusive LIFO free-stack —
3	//! the allocation half of the messaging layer, decoupling
4	//! "get a message" from "send it" (see the design doc's
5	//! "Messaging layer: pools and descriptor queues").
6	//!
7	//! - One `#[repr(C)]` [`PoolHeader`] spanning two cache lines
8	//!   (immutable geometry, free-stack head), followed by
9	//!   `buf_count` buffers of `buf_size` bytes each.
10	//! - The free-stack is intrusive with zero per-buffer
11	//!   overhead: a *free* buffer's first word holds the index
12	//!   of the next free buffer; an allocated buffer carries no
13	//!   header at all (pool-id provenance is deferred — see
14	//!   todo.md's In Progress block).
15	//! - One owning allocator pops ([`Pool::alloc`], `&mut self`
16	//!   as the in-process single-popper token); any holder
17	//!   frees ([`BufSlot::free`], the MPSC push side). The
18	//!   [`BufSlot`] guard does not borrow the pool, so any
19	//!   number of allocated buffers may be live at once —
20	//!   the decoupling the messaging layer exists for.
21	
22	use core::marker::PhantomData;
23	use core::mem::size_of;
24	use core::ops::{Deref, DerefMut};
25	use core::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, Ordering};
26	use zerocopy::{FromBytes, IntoBytes, KnownLayout};
27	
28	use crate::{CACHE_LINE_SIZE, CacheAligned, Error, check_type};
29	
30	/// `alloc` failed: no free buffer (or a peer-corrupted
31	/// free-stack index — validation fails toward exhaustion,
32	/// never toward handing out memory the pool doesn't own).
33	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
34	pub struct Exhausted;
35	
36	/// Layout marker written by [`Pool::init`]; rejects foreign
37	/// (including ring) regions on attach.
38	const POOL_MAGIC: u32 = 0x5A43_5031; // "ZCP1"
39	
40	/// Bumped on any change to the pool region layout.
41	const POOL_LAYOUT_VERSION: u32 = 1;
42	
43	/// Free-stack terminator: no next free buffer.
44	///
45	/// - Also why `buf_count < u32::MAX`: every valid index must
46	///   be distinguishable from the sentinel.
47	const NIL: u32 = u32::MAX;
48	
49	/// Control block at offset 0 of a pool region — two cache
50	/// lines: cold geometry, then the contended free-stack head.
51	///
52	/// - line 0: geometry, written by [`Pool::init`] with `magic`
53	///   last (`Release`), read-only thereafter. Cold: per-op
54	///   paths use the handle's snapshot.
55	/// - line 1: `first_free_idx`, CAS-contended by every freer and
56	///   the allocator — sole owner of its line.
57	/// - Every field is atomic for the same reason as the ring
58	///   [`Header`](crate::Header): a peer may be mapped at any
59	///   time, and scribbles must be garbage values, never UB.
60	#[repr(C)]
61	pub struct PoolHeader {
62	    /// Layout marker ([`POOL_MAGIC`]); stored last by init
63	    /// (`Release`), loaded first by attach (`Acquire`).
64	    magic: AtomicU32,
65	    /// Pool layout version ([`POOL_LAYOUT_VERSION`]).
66	    layout_version: AtomicU32,
67	    /// Buffer size in bytes — a [`CACHE_LINE_SIZE`] multiple.
68	    buf_size: AtomicU32,
69	    /// Buffer count — nonzero, `< u32::MAX` ([`NIL`]).
70	    buf_count: AtomicU32,
71	    /// [`CACHE_LINE_SIZE`] this region was built with; attach
72	    /// validates it like the rest of the geometry.
73	    cache_line_size: AtomicU32,
74	    /// Free-stack head: index of the first free buffer, or
75	    /// [`NIL`] when the pool is exhausted.
76	    first_free_idx: CacheAligned<AtomicU32>,
77	}
78	
79	const _: () = assert!(size_of::<PoolHeader>() == 2 * CACHE_LINE_SIZE);
80	
81	/// A validated view over a pool region.
82	///
83	/// - Geometry is snapshotted out of the header at
84	///   init/attach: per-op paths never re-read fields a peer
85	///   could scribble on. Free-stack links live in shared
86	///   buffer memory and are validated at every pop instead.
87	pub struct Pool<'a> {
88	    /// The region's control block.
89	    header: &'a PoolHeader,
90	    /// Base of the buffer array
91	    /// (offset `size_of::<PoolHeader>()`).
92	    bufs: *mut u8,
93	    /// Snapshot of `header.buf_size`.
94	    buf_size: u32,
95	    /// Snapshot of `header.buf_count`.
96	    buf_count: u32,
97	    _region: PhantomData<&'a [u8]>,
98	}
99	
100	// SAFETY: the handle owns the allocator role; the shared
101	// state it touches (first_free_idx, next-links) is atomic,
102	// and buffer ownership is handed off with Release/Acquire
103	// ordering — same rationale as the ring endpoints.
104	unsafe impl Send for Pool<'_> {}
105	
106	impl<'a> Pool<'a> {
107	    /// Initialize a fresh region and return the pool over it,
108	    /// with every buffer on the free-stack.
109	    ///
110	    /// - `buf_size` — bytes per buffer, a [`CACHE_LINE_SIZE`]
111	    ///   multiple.
112	    /// - `buf_count` — number of buffers, nonzero and

125	            return Err(Error::TooSmall);
126	        }
127	        // SAFETY: alignment + room for the PoolHeader checked
128	        // by pool_header_ptr; region is exclusively borrowed
129	        // for 'a; any byte pattern is a valid PoolHeader
130	        // (all-atomic fields, plain-byte padding).
131	        let header = unsafe { &*header };
132	        header
133	            .layout_version
134	            .store(POOL_LAYOUT_VERSION, Ordering::Relaxed);
135	        header.buf_size.store(buf_size, Ordering::Relaxed);
136	        header.buf_count.store(buf_count, Ordering::Relaxed);
137	        header
138	            .cache_line_size
139	            .store(CACHE_LINE_SIZE as u32, Ordering::Relaxed);
140	        // SAFETY: in bounds — len >= header + buffers.
141	        let bufs = unsafe { base.add(size_of::<PoolHeader>()) };
142	        let pool = Pool {

144	            bufs,
145	            buf_size,
146	            buf_count,
147	            _region: PhantomData,
148	        };

159	        pool.header.magic.store(POOL_MAGIC, Ordering::Release);

1	//! Zero-copy no_std SPSC ring buffer over a caller-provided
2	//! memory region, per [notes/ring-buffer-design.md]:
3	//!
4	//! - One `#[repr(C)]` [`Header`] spanning four cache lines
5	//!   (immutable geometry, producer index, consumer index, app
6	//!   user words), followed by M slots of N bytes.
7	//! - Indices are free-running `AtomicU32`s, masked only at slot
8	//!   access; full is `p - c == M`, no sacrificial slot.
9	//! - Messages move in place: the producer writes through a
10	//!   [`WriteSlot`] `&mut T`, the consumer reads through a
11	//!   [`ReadSlot`] `&T` — zerocopy traits bound `T`, no
12	//!   serialization step. Each side reserves at most one slot at
13	//!   a time; the guard holds the endpoint borrow until commit /
14	//!   release (or drop).
15	//!
16	//! [notes/ring-buffer-design.md]: https://github.com/winksaville/zc-ring-x1/blob/main/notes/ring-buffer-design.md
17	
18	#![cfg_attr(not(test), no_std)]
19	
20	use core::marker::PhantomData;
21	use core::mem::{align_of, size_of};
22	use core::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, Ordering};
23	
24	mod consumer;
25	mod pool;
26	mod producer;
27	
28	pub use consumer::{Consumer, Empty, ReadSlot};
29	pub use pool::{BufSlot, Exhausted, Pool, PoolHeader};
30	pub use producer::{Full, Producer, WriteSlot};
31	
32	/// Cache-line size the layout is built around.
33	///
34	/// - Slot size must be a multiple of this; slots and the region
35	///   itself must be aligned to it.
36	pub const CACHE_LINE_SIZE: usize = 64;
37	
38	/// Layout marker written by [`Ring::init`]; rejects foreign
39	/// regions on attach.
40	const MAGIC: u32 = 0x5A43_5231; // "ZCR1"
41	
42	/// Bumped on any change to the region layout.
43	const LAYOUT_VERSION: u32 = 2;
44	
45	/// Number of `AtomicU32` words in the header's app-owned
46	/// `user` line.
47	pub const USER_WORDS: usize = 16;
48	
49	/// Cache-line-aligned wrapper granting its field sole
50	/// ownership of the line.
51	///
52	/// - `repr(align(N))` accepts only an integer literal — it
53	///   cannot name [`CACHE_LINE_SIZE`] — so the `64` is written out
54	///   and a const assert ties them back together.
55	#[repr(C, align(64))]
56	struct CacheAligned<T>(T);
57	
58	const _: () = assert!(align_of::<CacheAligned<AtomicU32>>() == CACHE_LINE_SIZE);
59	
60	impl<T> core::ops::Deref for CacheAligned<T> {
61	    type Target = T;
62	    /// Access the wrapped value.
63	    fn deref(&self) -> &T {
64	        &self.0
65	    }
66	}
67	
68	/// Control block at offset 0 of the region — one struct spanning
69	/// four cache lines so each concurrently-written index owns its
70	/// own line.
71	///
72	/// - line 0: geometry, written by [`Ring::init`] with `magic`
73	///   last (`Release`), read-only thereafter. Cold: per-op
74	///   paths use the handles' snapshots.
75	/// - line 1: `producer_idx`, written only by the producer.
76	/// - line 2: `consumer_idx`, written only by the consumer.
77	/// - line 3: `user`, app-owned scratch — last, so an app
78	///   overrun walks into its own slot data, not an index line.
79	/// - Every field is atomic: the region may be mapped by a peer
80	///   at any time, so even "immutable" fields must be free of
81	///   data races. `AtomicU32` has the same layout as `u32`, so
82	///   this does not affect `layout_version`.
83	#[repr(C)]
84	pub struct Header {
85	    /// Layout marker ([`MAGIC`]); stored last by init
86	    /// (`Release`), loaded first by attach (`Acquire`), so a
87	    /// peer that observes it also observes the geometry.
88	    magic: AtomicU32,
89	    /// Layout version ([`LAYOUT_VERSION`]).
90	    layout_version: AtomicU32,
91	    /// Slot size N in bytes — a [`CACHE_LINE_SIZE`] multiple.
92	    slot_size: AtomicU32,
93	    /// Slot count M — a power of two `<= 2^31`.
94	    capacity: AtomicU32,
95	    /// [`CACHE_LINE_SIZE`] this region was built with. The line size
96	    /// is a layout parameter (padding, offsets, slot granule),
97	    /// so builds must agree; attach validates it like the rest
98	    /// of the geometry.
99	    cache_line_size: AtomicU32,
100	    /// Free-running count of messages committed.
101	    producer_idx: CacheAligned<AtomicU32>,
102	    /// Free-running count of messages released.
103	    consumer_idx: CacheAligned<AtomicU32>,
104	    /// App-owned scratch line ([`USER_WORDS`] words): zeroed by
105	    /// init, then never read, written, or interpreted by the
106	    /// crate. See the design doc's "Blocking and user words".
107	    user: CacheAligned<[AtomicU32; USER_WORDS]>,
108	}
109	
110	const _: () = assert!(size_of::<Header>() == 4 * CACHE_LINE_SIZE);
111	
112	/// Errors from region validation — [`Ring::init`] /
113	/// [`Ring::attach`] and [`Pool::init`] / [`Pool::attach`].
114	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
115	pub enum Error {
116	    /// Region is not [`CACHE_LINE_SIZE`]-aligned.
117	    Misaligned,
118	    /// Region is smaller than header + slots/buffers.
119	    TooSmall,
120	    /// Slot size is zero or not a [`CACHE_LINE_SIZE`] multiple.
121	    BadSlotSize,
122	    /// Capacity is zero, not a power of two, or `> 2^31`.
123	    BadCapacity,
124	    /// Pool: buffer size is zero or not a [`CACHE_LINE_SIZE`]
125	    /// multiple.
126	    BadBufSize,
127	    /// Pool: buffer count is zero or `u32::MAX` (the
128	    /// free-stack NIL sentinel).
129	    BadBufCount,
130	    /// Attach: magic mismatch — not a region of the expected
131	    /// kind.
132	    BadMagic,
133	    /// Attach: layout version mismatch.
134	    BadLayoutVersion,
135	    /// Attach: region built with a different [`CACHE_LINE_SIZE`].
136	    BadCacheLine,
137	}
138	
139	/// A validated view over a ring region; split into the two
140	/// endpoint handles with [`Ring::split`].
141	///
142	/// - Geometry is snapshotted out of the header at
143	///   init/attach: per-op paths never re-read fields a peer
144	///   could scribble on, so a hostile peer can degrade the ring
145	///   to garbage messages or spurious Full/Empty, never to UB.
146	pub struct Ring<'a> {
147	    /// The region's control block.
148	    header: &'a Header,
149	    /// Base of the slot array (offset `size_of::<Header>()`).
150	    slots: *mut u8,
151	    /// Snapshot of `header.slot_size`.
152	    slot_size: u32,
153	    /// Snapshot of `header.capacity`.
154	    capacity: u32,
155	    /// Slot-position mask (`capacity - 1`).
156	    mask: u32,
157	    _region: PhantomData<&'a [u8]>,
158	}
159	
160	impl<'a> Ring<'a> {
161	    /// Initialize a fresh region and return the ring over it.
162	    ///
163	    /// - `slot_size` — N bytes per slot, a [`CACHE_LINE_SIZE`]
164	    ///   multiple.
165	    /// - `capacity` — M slots, a power of two `<= 2^31`.
166	    /// - The region must be [`CACHE_LINE_SIZE`]-aligned and at least
167	    ///   `size_of::<Header>() + M * N` bytes.
168	    pub fn init(region: &'a mut [u8], slot_size: u32, capacity: u32) -> Result<Self, Error> {
169	        validate_geometry(slot_size, capacity)?;
170	        let len = region.len();
171	        // Taken exactly once: a second `as_mut_ptr()` would
172	        // retag the slice and invalidate `header` under Stacked
173	        // Borrows (Miri-verified).
174	        let base = region.as_mut_ptr();
175	        let header = header_ptr(base, len)?;
176	        if (len as u64) < region_size(slot_size, capacity) {
177	            return Err(Error::TooSmall);
178	        }
179	        // SAFETY: alignment + room for the Header checked by
180	        // header_ptr; region is exclusively borrowed for 'a;
181	        // any byte pattern is a valid Header (all-atomic
182	        // fields, plain-byte padding).
183	        let header = unsafe { &*header };
184	        header
185	            .layout_version
186	            .store(LAYOUT_VERSION, Ordering::Relaxed);
187	        header.slot_size.store(slot_size, Ordering::Relaxed);
188	        header.capacity.store(capacity, Ordering::Relaxed);
189	        header
190	            .cache_line_size
191	            .store(CACHE_LINE_SIZE as u32, Ordering::Relaxed);
192	        header.producer_idx.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
193	        header.consumer_idx.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);

... [391 lines truncated] ...

Both renames are in, all 18 tests pass under `cargo test` and Miri:

- **`CACHE_LINE_SIZE`** — crate-wide: the pub const in `lib.rs` (19 sites), all of `pool.rs` (16), and the design doc's three references (kept in sync per its charter). README had no code-level references. The `repr(align(64))` literals stay, tied back by the existing const asserts — that limitation note in `CacheAligned`'s doc now reads `CACHE_LINE_SIZE` too. The header fields, the constant, and the attach check now all speak one name.
- **`TEST_CACHE_LINE`** — the pool tests' const (17 sites), with its doc comment still stating its role ("buffer size used by most pool tests: one cache line").

One boundary note: the ring's own tests in `lib.rs` still use literal `64`s in their `Ring::init` calls — untouched since the sweep you asked for was the pool tests. A matching cleanup there is mechanical if you want it in this commit; otherwise `-4` is complete and ready for its description.

content.text:
Description for `0.6.0-4`:

```
test: message pool protocol tests

Eleven pool tests written against the README usage model —
geometry/attach validation, the decoupling itself (many
live guards), LIFO reuse, leak-on-drop, corruption failing
toward Exhausted, type edges (mixed sizes in one pool,
internal alignment, too-big panics, ZST consumes a
buffer), and a threaded allocator/freer role split. All
pass under cargo test and Miri. Naming: CACHE_LINE →
CACHE_LINE_SIZE crate-wide, matching the header fields.

- src/pool.rs
  - tests: init_rejects_bad_geometry (incl. 32-bit wrap
    tripwire), attach_validates_header (uninitialized and
    ring regions both BadMagic — kinds cannot
    cross-attach; BadCacheLine), alloc_holds_many_then_
    exhausts, free_is_lifo (pointer identity),
    dropped_guard_leaks_its_buffer,
    corrupted_stack_fails_toward_exhausted (head and
    next-link arms, repair restores service),
    wider_buffers_and_mixed_types (2-line buffers, reuse
    as a different type), internally_aligned_types_work
    (align(32)), too_big_type_panics (should_panic via
    check_type), zero_sized_type_consumes_a_buffer,
    threaded_alloc_here_free_there (guards cross a
    channel; 100k msgs through 4 buffers, 200 under Miri)
  - unsafe impl Send for Pool (allocator role moves across
    threads; surfaced by the threaded test)
  - alloc doc: geometry panics on mismatch; ZST legal,
    buffer is the allocation granule
  - TEST_CACHE_LINE_SIZE const replaces literal 64s in
    tests
- src/lib.rs: CACHE_LINE renamed CACHE_LINE_SIZE (pub
  const; all references)
- notes/ring-buffer-design.md: rename synced (3 sites)
- notes/todo.md: ladder -4 (done)
- notes/chores/chores-01.md: backfill [[18]] (the 0.6.0-3
  docs SHA) into the message-pool section
- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version-of-record 0.6.0-4
```

Approve and I'll run `vc-x1 push main`.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
Write down who may do what before the tests enforce it:
roles (ring: one producer + one consumer; pool: one
allocator + open freeing), the buffer lifecycle (free →
allocated → in-flight [vacant until descriptor queues] →
freed) with one permitted toucher per state, what "send"
means this cycle (moving the BufSlot), and where it heads —
descriptors on the ring, payloads at rest in pools, bytes
written once and never copied. Lives in the README as the
user-facing contract; the design doc points at it.

- README.md
  - new "Message pool" section: overview bullets (BufSlot
    ownership token, single-popper alloc / MPSC free,
    init/attach + single-allocator contract, standalone
    object-allocator value) + runnable example
  - new "Usage model: roles and buffer lifecycle" section,
    closing with an illustrative (marked not-yet-real)
    sketch of the composed pool + ring descriptor flow
  - Testing: pool_readme example listed
- examples/pool_readme.rs: new — the README pool snippet,
  clippy-kept compiling; first live exercise of alloc/free
- notes/ring-buffer-design.md
  - messaging-layer intro: pool half marked as-built (0.6.0)
  - usage-model subsection reduced to a pointer + summary
- notes/todo.md
  - ladder -3 (done)
  - Ideas: BufSlot auto-free on Drop (RAII trade-off);
    global_allocator experiment over size-class pools
    (needs phase-2 shared alloc or per-thread routing)
- notes/chores/chores-01.md: backfill [[17]] (the 0.6.0-2
  feat SHA) into the message-pool section
- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version-of-record 0.6.0-3
